Final body ~30 LOC, at the low end of the iter-141 30–50 LOC envelope.

attempts.insight: Confirms a NEW finding: even the `_=0`-shape `change` with a `_` LHS placeholder triggers the `pushforward₀` `whnf` timeout when the elided LHS carries `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv.symm` — `whnf` is forced to elaborate the `_` and descends. The iter-140 + iter-142 lesson is now strengthened: `change` placeholders are unsafe whenever ANY side of the equation crosses `pushforward₀`-annotated definitions, even on `_=0`-shape goals where iter-140 expected the `whnf` to short-circuit. The safe pattern is fully-explicit `change` blocks (no `_`).
